race.name: GFNY Punta del Este
race.tagline: 146km of beach glamour meets brutal hills in Uruguay's sexiest sufferfest.

vitals.location: Punta del Este, Maldonado Department, Uruguay
vitals.field_size: Intimate GFNY field of hundreds, drawing South American elites and sun-seeking internationals
vitals.registration: Entry locks in elite chip timing, mandatory NYC-designed Italian jersey, and finisher's medal toward multi-event glory
vitals.feed_zones: Fully stocked GFNY feeds with post-race beach club feast and awards
vitals.cutoff_time: Sag wagon sweeps ensure all finishers medal, no DNF shame
route_options: Long Competitive: 90.7mi, 5423ft — Hilly beast for elites chasing qualifiers

climate.description: March delivers Punta del Este's steamy Southern Hemisphere sendoff with temps pushing 85°F and relentless coastal winds. Late summer heat bakes the beaches while ocean breezes offer fleeting mercy. Hydration is non-negotiable amid the glamour.

terrain.primary: Flat-to-hilly coastal crusher with wind-whipped drama
terrain.surface: Silky paved paradise from beachfront boulevards to smooth inland climbs. Police escort tames traffic for pro-peloton flow. Luxury villa views mask the sneaky suffering.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 71. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 66.

course_description.character: Playa Mansa erupts into 22km coastal flats before El Chorro's 800m hammer splits the bunch. Pine forests and wild beaches yield to Punta Ballena's iconic sting, looping back via Piriápolis oceanway. Interbalnearia finale panoramas reward survivors amid luxury ranches and lakes—windy, wavy, wildly scenic road poetry.
course_description.signature_challenge: Punta Ballena's mid-race bite atop early El Chorro damage—where groups shatter and solo quests ignite. Winds amplify the isolation, turning paradise into personal hell.

logistics.airport: Fly into Punta del Este's PDP for 20-min beach transfers or Montevideo's MVD for budget hauls. Directs from Miami/Buenos Aires keep it painless.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Crash beachfront at Parador Pedro Figari or AWA Boutique—walkable to start/expo. Book early for March peak; Airbnbs slash costs inland.
logistics.food: Uruguayan parrilladas fuel the fire—stock up on asado and malbec. Race feeds handle the ride; post-beach feasts await victors.

history.origin_story: Launched in 2018 to conquer South America's glitziest coast, GFNY Punta del Este imported NYC's pro-for-a-day ethos to Uruguay's Pearl of the Atlantic. Beach starts met inland hills, drawing LatAm riders hungry for qualifiers. Evolving courses cemented it as a Southern summer classic, blending luxury with lung-burning reality.
history.reputation: GFNY's beach-circuit crown jewel for wind-battered warriors. Riders rave about the vibe; elites target for regional hardware.

biased_opinion.summary: GFNY Punta del Este fuses St. Tropez glamour with sneaky South American hills, turning beach bums into qualifiers. Winds howl off Playa Mansa while Punta Ballena snaps the elastic—pure pro drama in paradise. Redesigned yearly, it's the GFNY you didn't know you craved. Families lounge while you suffer; post-race feasts seal the legend. Skip if flats bore you—this undulating beast demands respect.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Exotic GFNY gem for early-season glory. Pin it if you crave coastal crushers; dodge if mountains are your mistress.

final_verdict.one_liner: Uruguay's hilly beach party punches way above its vert.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you're chasing qualifiers in sun-soaked style or opening North American legs jet-lag free. Skip if deep fields or mega climbs define your fix—stick to Euros for that.
final_verdict.alternatives: GFNY Punta Cana for Caribbean flats, GFNY Cozumel for island rollers, GFNY Los Cabos for Mexico heat.
